QF.id: VIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_10

question.text: How does the standardization of futures contracts contribute to market efficiency?

answer.text: Standardization ensures all market participants trade identical contract specifications, improving liquidity, enabling transparent price discovery, and facilitating easy entry and exit
Standardization allows each trader to customize the contract terms to match their specific hedging needs
Standardization means only large institutional investors can participate, ensuring higher quality counterparties
Standardization eliminates the need for margins, reducing the cost of trading futures

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** When all contracts have the same specifications (lot size, expiry, tick size, etc.), many participants trade the same product. This creates deep liquidity, narrow bid-ask spreads, transparent price discovery, and easy entry/exit. A trader who wants to close their position simply takes an opposite trade.
**The Trap:** Standardization means traders CANNOT customize terms (that is the trade-off compared to forwards). Retail investors CAN participate in futures. Margins are REQUIRED in futures, not eliminated.
**Cross-Exam Context:** The liquidity advantage of standardized futures over customized forwards is a key concept in Series VIII.
**Key Takeaway:** Standardization -> Fungibility -> Liquidity -> Efficient price discovery.
